2605.12691.title: On the Size Complexity and Decidability of First-Order Progression
2605.12691.paper_summary: Progression, the task of updating a knowledge base to reflect action effects, generally requires second-order logic. Identifying first-order special cases, by restricting either the knowledge base or action effects, has long been a central topic in reasoning about actions. It is known that local-effect, normal, and acyclic actions, three increasingly expressive classes, admit first-order progression. However, a systematic analysis of the size of such progressions, crucial for practical applications, has been missing. In this paper, using the framework of Situation Calculus, we show that under reasonable assumptions, first-order progression for these action classes grows only polynomially. Moreover, we show that when the KB belongs to decidable fragments such as two-variable first-order logic or universal theories with constants, the progression remains within the same fragment, ensuring decidability and practical applicability.
2605.12691.paper_summary_zh: 進展，即更新知識庫以反映行動效果的任務，通常需要二階邏輯。通過限制知識庫或行動效果來識別一階特殊情況，長久以來一直是推理行動的核心主題。已知局部效果、正常和無環行動這三個日益表達豐富的類別，允許一階進展。然而，對於這些進展的大小進行系統分析，對於實際應用至關重要，但這方面的研究一直缺失。在本文中，我們利用情境演算的框架，顯示在合理的假設下，這些行動類別的一階進展僅以多項式增長。此外，我們還顯示當知識庫屬於可判定片段，如兩變數一階邏輯或具有常數的全稱理論時，進展仍然保持在同一片段內，確保可判定性和實際應用性。
2605.12691.author: Jens Classen et.al.
